description:
You are an energy auditor evaluating a residential property. The homeowner currently leaves their thermostat at a constant 22°C year-round. Run an annual building energy analysis to calculate the energy savings of upgrading to a smart thermostat with an 'Eco' setback schedule.

1. Ensure the building's location/city is set to 'Boston, MA'.
2. Run a baseline 'Annual Building Energy Analysis' with both the Heating and Cooling Setpoints at 22°C. Note the 'Total' annual energy consumption (kWh) from the graph.
3. Change the building's thermostat to the proposed Eco configuration: Heating Setpoint = 18°C, Cooling Setpoint = 26°C.
4. Run the 'Annual Building Energy Analysis' again and note the new 'Total' consumption.
5. Calculate the absolute energy savings (Baseline - Eco) and the percentage savings.
6. Create a text file at ~/Documents/Energy3D/thermostat_savings_report.txt clearly listing: Baseline Energy, Eco Energy, Absolute Savings (kWh), and Percentage Savings.
7. Save the modified Energy3D model with the Eco settings applied as ~/Documents/Energy3D/eco_building.ng3.
